metadata.title: How to Invest Through Technology Cycles

conclusion: Sacerdote's bottom line: AI is earlier than the headlines suggest. Enterprise usage is still under 1%, compute is tight, and three model companies are pulling away. He favors Anthropic and infrastructure, doubts legacy software can charge enough for AI, and is hunting for overlooked hardware suppliers.
background: Alex Sacerdote left Fidelity about twenty years ago — Peter Lynch was a mentor — and built Whale Rock into a $17B Boston shop that's compounded roughly 44% over the last three years. Ten people, thousands of meetings a year, many with private companies. Patrick has him walk through the same S-curve lens he used for internet, mobile, and cloud, applied to AI. Anthropic is the name he keeps coming back to. The through-line: adoption feels tiny even when every CIO says they're 'doing AI,' and Whale Rock entered 2026 net short traditional application software.
important_facts: Whale Rock bought into Anthropic in August 2025 at ~$180B; revenue had gone from ~$100M to ~$1B with ~$9B the next target. Power users were spending up to ~$100/day on tokens (~$20–30K/year). With ~20M coders globally, Sacerdote puts the coding market alone at $500B+. Compute is sold out worldwide.

Anthropic has ~14–15M daily users. Sundar Pichai said ~10 bps of knowledge workers use AI in a deep way. Enterprise app-layer AI is under 1% penetrated. Anthropic still has only ~half the compute it wants. A 90-page internal deck on Claude Code helped seal the August investment.

Whale Rock bought Nvidia in 2023 at ~4x earnings, Tesla in 2019 at ~5x, Apple at ~4x. Software was ~40–50% of the book five years ago; entering 2026 they were net short app software. Of ~60 model startups at a 2023 industry webinar, almost all are gone. On chips he scores names by AI sales mix × share — 30% × 30% = 60 is the bar.

mental_model.name: S-Curve × Competitive Advantage × Underappreciated Earnings Power
mental_model.components: Find the adoption curve — sometimes an 'L-curve' when friction drops fast. Ask who has a real moat: network effects, scale, IP, lock-in. Then model earnings a few years out while the market still thinks linearly. On chips he multiplies AI revenue mix by AI market share; 60+ is the sweet spot.
mental_model.application: This cycle he went chips first (2023), then Anthropic after the 2025 coding inflection, sold SaaS when AI SKUs didn't move revenue, and dug into hardware via scuttlebutt — Celestica at ~8x as sole Google TPU box supplier. He tries to sell around 30–40% penetration; says he sold Apple too early at ~50% US smartphone share in 2012.

key_insights.view: At an April 2023 webinar he counted ~60 model startups; most are dead now, and it looks like a three-way race — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google — the way cloud did.
key_insights.question: Why wouldn't the model layer just commoditize?
key_insights.answer: Quality at the frontier still matters — 80% to 85% on benchmarks is a big jump, not a rounding error. Open source trails without compute. Revenue really clicked when coding took off; Claude Code in mid-2025 was the moment they leaned in.

key_insights.view: Salesforce does ~$40B a year; its AI ARR is maybe $500–700M. That's the gap he's watching — AI is 1–2% of sales for most software names, not the 20%+ the old 'rule of 40' math would need.
key_insights.question: Can incumbents like Salesforce actually make money on AI?
key_insights.answer: Not much evidence yet — AI products barely register on the P&L. They went net short app software entering 2026. He leaves room for a bull case: plug Claude into Slack, let agents live inside CRM. But he said that's still half-baked.

key_insights.view: Data-center hardware was a commodity for forty years under Intel x86. AI workloads growing ~10x a year are forcing real engineering again — HBM, boards, liquid cooling.
key_insights.question: Why are suppliers like Celestica suddenly interesting?
key_insights.answer: An AI server runs $200–300K versus $5K for the old kind — and it can't break. Celestica was the sole Google TPU supplier at ~8x earnings when they found it. Sequoia's Shawn Maguire told him he wished he could run a hardware fund again.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Whale Rock entered around $180B as revenue scaled from ~$100M to ~$1B; Claude Code and enterprise tooling could create AWS-like lock-in
AI infrastructure remains capacity constrained; Nvidia benefits across model winners as compute demand stays sold out
Whale Rock entered 2026 net short app software because AI revenue is often only ~1–2% of sales while CIO budgets shift toward model tokens

golden_quotes: When you get the right part of the S-curve, unit growth goes exponential, and the world still tends to think linearly.
Enterprise AI is less than 1% penetrated; Sacerdote says the curve may look less like an S and more like an L going straight up.
He compared old software workflows to a horse and buggy and AI-native workflows to the transporter from Star Trek.

events.event: Fidelity analyst; mentored by Peter Lynch
Father (Goldman Sachs exec, firm chairman) passes after 6 years
Nvidia at ~4× earnings; ~60 AI model startups at industry webinar
Anthropic investment at ~$180B valuation (~$100M→$1B revenue run-rate)
$17B+ AUM; ~44% 3-yr CAGR; net short application software entering year
